text:
I'm creating a job search accountability group of 4-6 people. The purpose is to do 1 practical problem a day (frontend/backend/sql/system design). possibly leetcode as well, depends on everyone's goals.

We will also do morning daily standups and make sure we exercise regularly! North American timing.

If anyone wants to join the group please dm me a short intro of yourself and your discord username. thanks
label: OTHER

text:
FLC application essentially means a company certifies to the federal govt, specifically DOL that "I am having a hard time finding software engineers, so please give this candidate a green card so they can come work for us". DOL almost always comes back with "Okay, whatever you say"

Data from: [https://www.dol.gov/agencies/eta/foreign-labor/performance](https://www.dol.gov/agencies/eta/foreign-labor/performance)

last 3 months 2025

    Bloomberg L.P.         89  
    Yahoo Holdings Inc.    82  
    Oracle America, Inc.   80  
    JPMORGAN CHASE & CO.   75  
    Meta Platforms, Inc.   53  
    Salesforce, Inc.       51  
    Microsoft Corporation  49  
    
    ..and many others.
label: OTHER

text:
I keep seeing people say they regret quitting and that it’s more stressful to make less money, but I’m at my breaking point and have been for a while. I have savings, dual income household, and can freelance. I just really want some time to focus on my family and my mental health. My job is ruining me mentally and seeping into the rest of my life. 

I’m hesitant because of the job market, but I’m so anxious and stressed out, I don’t know how much longer I can keep doing this. I do not want it to get to the point where it’s harming my physical health as well.
label: OTHER

text:
I have $40K in my savings(HYSA @ $4.99%) with $30K left on the loan. The interest rate of the car loan is 3.99%. I'm able to survive on my pay checks and always put at least half of my paycheck back into my savings.

So I will still have at least 3 months of expenses saved up if I do plan on paying off my car early.
label: OTHER

text: I have a 70k gross salary and will be relocating to a job in a HCOL area. I have no loans or car payments, and the apartment is very convenient. But, I also want to be responsible since this is my first apartment and real job.
label: OTHER

text:
Title says it all.

Backstory: We always buy new, but drive our cars for 10+ years. Because of this, we’ve always taken advantage of 0 or near 0% loan rates.

Spouse’s car was rear ended and totaled. So they found a good used car that’s 1yr old.  Insurance is payout will roll right into the new loan but only covers 60% of the cost.  Now exploring financing options.

Appreciate the community’s insights.

Edit: already checked primary FI (6%) and credit union (5.3%)
label: OTHER

text:
I get the sense that your whole life had to revolve around medicine to enjoy being a doctor. I have a ton of interests in life, medicine is certainly one high on the list. I also enjoy computers, painting, music, athletics. I don’t feel like I’m a person who’s shouting at the top of my lungs I LOVE MEDICINE ITS MY WHOLE LIFE PURPOSE. But I do think doctor is an amazing job & very intellectually stimulating. It would be the best job in the planet.

That being said, I can understand how for especially during residency and even parts of med school, your life will basically revolve around nothing but medicine.
label: OTHER

text: Yes, based on my experience, I would definitely recommend Henry Harvin institute to others. The learning environment is supportive, the trainers are knowledgeable, and the overall guidance helps in skill development and career growth. It provides practical knowledge and confidence to move forward successfully.
label: OTHER

text:
Hi everyone,

If you are trying to build a career in law, you probably already know how demanding and competitive the sector can feel. Between the SQE, applications, qualifying work experience and endless advice online, it is easy to feel lost or unsure about your next steps.

I work at BARBRI, supporting aspiring solicitors as they navigate the SQE pathway and the wider legal careers landscape. I speak with people every day who are trying to balance ambition with uncertainty. The truth is that there is no single correct way into law and you do not need a flawless background to succeed. With the right guidance, strategy and confidence, you can absolutely build a future in the profession.

If you have any questions, drop them here.

Looking forward to supporting you.
label: OTHER
